 {"id":"beads_rust-pzr","title":"Implement structured JSON error output","description":"# Structured JSON Error Output

## Purpose
Provide AI coding agents with structured, machine-parseable error information that includes error codes, hints, and retryability flags for intelligent error handling.

## Technical Requirements

### Error Output Format
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "ISSUE_NOT_FOUND",
    "message": "Issue 'bd-xyz123' not found",
    "hint": "Did you mean 'bd-xyz12'? Use 'br list' to see all issues.",
    "retryable": false,
    "context": {
      "searched_id": "bd-xyz123",
      "similar_ids": ["bd-xyz12", "bd-xyz1"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Error Code Enum
```rust
pub enum ErrorCode {
    // Database errors
    DatabaseNotFound,
    DatabaseCorrupted,
    NotInitialized,
    
    // Issue errors
    IssueNotFound,
    AmbiguousId,
    DuplicateId,
    
    // Validation errors
    InvalidPriority,
    InvalidStatus,
    InvalidIssueType,
    TitleRequired,
    
    // Dependency errors
    CycleDetected,
    DependencyNotFound,
    
    // Sync errors
    JsonlParseError,
    ConflictMarkers,
    PathTraversal,
    
    // Config errors
    ConfigNotFound,
    ConfigParseError,
}

impl ErrorCode {
    pub fn as_str(\u0026self) -> \u0026'static str { ... }
    pub fn is_retryable(\u0026self) -> bool { ... }
    pub fn exit_code(\u0026self) -> i32 { ... }
}
```

### Context-Aware Hints
- IssueNotFound: suggest similar IDs using Levenshtein distance
- NotInitialized: suggest 'br init'
- InvalidPriority: show valid range
- CycleDetected: show the cycle path

### Implementation
```rust
pub struct StructuredError {
    pub code: ErrorCode,
    pub message: String,
    pub hint: Option<String>,
    pub retryable: bool,
    pub context: Option<serde_json::Value>,
}

impl StructuredError {
    pub fn to_json(\u0026self) -> serde_json::Value { ... }
    pub fn to_human(\u0026self, color: bool) -> String { ... }
}
```

### TTY Detection
- If stdout is TTY and not --json: human-readable colored output
- If stdout is pipe or --json: structured JSON
- Always write errors to stderr

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All errors have unique error codes
- [ ] Error output is valid JSON when --json flag used
- [ ] Hints are context-aware and helpful
- [ ] Retryable flag is accurate
- [ ] Similar ID suggestions work for IssueNotFound
- [ ] Exit codes are consistent

## Dependencies
- Error module enhancement (existing)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ScarletAnchor","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T18:49:49.34493334Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T20:29:47.627417656Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T20:29:47.627354156Z","close_reason":"Implemented structured JSON error output with: ErrorCode enum (30+ codes), StructuredError struct with to_json/to_human, Levenshtein-based ID suggestions, intent detection for status/type/priority, context-aware hints, TTY detection for output mode, 22 unit tests, 8 E2E structured error tests. All tests pass."}
 {"id":"beads_rust-ql7","title":"config Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br config` command for viewing and modifying configuration settings. Configuration is stored per-project in the .beads directory.

## CLI Interface
```
br config <COMMAND>

Commands:
  get <KEY>                   Get a config value
  set <KEY> <VALUE>           Set a config value
  list                        List all config values
  reset <KEY>                 Reset to default
  init                        Create default config

Options:
  --global                    Use global (~/.config/br/) config
  --json                      Output as JSON
```

## Configuration Keys

### Core Settings
```toml
# .beads/config.toml (or stored in SQLite config table)

[core]
prefix = "beads_rust"         # ID prefix for new issues
default_priority = 2          # Default priority for new issues
default_type = "task"         # Default type for new issues

[display]
color = true                  # Enable colored output
date_format = "relative"      # "relative", "iso", "local"
truncate_titles = 80          # Max title length in list view

[sync]
auto_flush = false            # Auto-export after changes (non-invasive default)
auto_import = true            # Auto-import if JSONL is newer

[user]
name = ""                     # Override for commit author
email = ""                    # Override for commit email
```

### Implementation
```rust
struct Config {
    core: CoreConfig,
    display: DisplayConfig,
    sync: SyncConfig,
    user: UserConfig,
}

impl Config {
    fn load(beads_dir: &Path) -> Result<Self> {
        // Try config.toml first
        let config_path = beads_dir.join("config.toml");
        if config_path.exists() {
            return Self::from_toml(&config_path);
        }
        
        // Fall back to SQLite config table
        let db_path = beads_dir.join("*.db");
        if let Some(db) = find_db(&beads_dir)? {
            return Self::from_sqlite(&db);
        }
        
        Ok(Self::default())
    }
    
    fn get(&self, key: &str) -> Option<String> {
        match key {
            "core.prefix" => Some(self.core.prefix.clone()),
            "display.color" => Some(self.display.color.to_string()),
            // ... etc
            _ => None,
        }
    }
}
```

### Database Schema
```sql
CREATE TABLE config (
    key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    value TEXT NOT NULL,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
);
```

## Output Formats

### List (Human-readable)
```
Configuration for .beads/:

[core]
  prefix = beads_rust
  default_priority = 2
  default_type = task

[display]
  color = true
  date_format = relative

[sync]
  auto_flush = false
  auto_import = true
```

### JSON
```json
{
  "core": {
    "prefix": "beads_rust",
    "default_priority": 2
  },
  "display": {
    "color": true
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Get individual config values
- [ ] Set config values
- [ ] List all config values
- [ ] Reset to defaults
- [ ] Support global config (~/.config/br/)
- [ ] Merge global + project config (project wins)
- [ ] Validate config values
- [ ] Human-readable and JSON output

## Dependencies
- Requires init Command (config location)
- Requires SQLite Storage Layer (config table)

## Rationale
Configuration allows customization without modifying code. Global config handles user preferences (color, name); project config handles project-specific settings (prefix). The non-invasive defaults (auto_flush = false) align with br's philosophy.

 {"id":"beads_rust-qlh","title":"search Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br search` command for full-text search across issues. This enables finding issues by content when you don't know exact IDs or titles.

## CLI Interface
```
br search [OPTIONS] <QUERY>

Arguments:
  <QUERY>  Search query (supports basic operators)

Options:
  -s, --status <STATUS>     Limit to status (open, closed, all)
  -t, --type <TYPE>         Limit to type
  --fields <FIELDS>         Fields to search (title, description, comments)
  --limit <N>               Max results (default: 20)
  --json                    Output as JSON
  --robot                   Machine-readable output
```

## Implementation Details

### SQLite FTS5 Setup
```sql
-- Full-text search virtual table
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE issues_fts USING fts5(
    id,
    title,
    description,
    content='issues',
    content_rowid='rowid',
    tokenize='porter unicode61'
);

-- Triggers to keep FTS in sync
CREATE TRIGGER issues_ai AFTER INSERT ON issues BEGIN
    INSERT INTO issues_fts(rowid, id, title, description)
    VALUES (new.rowid, new.id, new.title, new.description);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER issues_ad AFTER DELETE ON issues BEGIN
    INSERT INTO issues_fts(issues_fts, rowid, id, title, description)
    VALUES ('delete', old.rowid, old.id, old.title, old.description);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER issues_au AFTER UPDATE ON issues BEGIN
    INSERT INTO issues_fts(issues_fts, rowid, id, title, description)
    VALUES ('delete', old.rowid, old.id, old.title, old.description);
    INSERT INTO issues_fts(rowid, id, title, description)
    VALUES (new.rowid, new.id, new.title, new.description);
END;
```

### Search Implementation
```rust
fn search(&self, query: &str, opts: SearchOptions) -> Result<Vec<SearchResult>> {
    // Use FTS5 MATCH syntax
    let sql = r#"
        SELECT 
            i.*,
            highlight(issues_fts, 1, '<mark>', '</mark>') as title_highlight,
            highlight(issues_fts, 2, '<mark>', '</mark>') as desc_highlight,
            bm25(issues_fts) as rank
        FROM issues_fts
        JOIN issues i ON issues_fts.id = i.id
        WHERE issues_fts MATCH ?
        ORDER BY rank
        LIMIT ?
    "#;
    
    // FTS5 query syntax:
    // - Simple terms: "authentication"
    // - Phrase: "user authentication"
    // - OR: "auth OR login"
    // - NOT: "auth NOT oauth"
    // - Prefix: "auth*"
}
```

### Comment Search
Optionally search comments as well:
```sql
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE comments_fts USING fts5(
    issue_id,
    body,
    content='comments',
    content_rowid='rowid',
    tokenize='porter unicode61'
);
```

## Output Formats

### Human-readable
```
Search results for "authentication" (5 matches):

[P0] beads_rust-abc123  Implement user <mark>authentication</mark>
     "...JWT-based <mark>authentication</mark> with refresh tokens..."

[P1] beads_rust-def456  Fix <mark>authentication</mark> timeout bug
     "...session expires during <mark>authentication</mark> flow..."

[P2] beads_rust-ghi789  Document <mark>authentication</mark> API
     "...describes the <mark>authentication</mark> endpoints..."
```

### JSON
```json
{
  "query": "authentication",
  "results": [
    {
      "id": "beads_rust-abc123",
      "title": "Implement user authentication",
      "rank": 0.95,
      "highlights": {
        "title": "Implement user <mark>authentication</mark>",
        "description": "...JWT-based <mark>authentication</mark>..."
      }
    }
  ],
  "count": 5
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Full-text search across titles
- [ ] Full-text search across descriptions
- [ ] Optional comment search
- [ ] Support FTS5 query operators (phrase, OR, NOT, prefix)
- [ ] Rank results by relevance (BM25)
- [ ] Highlight matching terms
- [ ] Filter by status/type
- [ ] Limit results
- [ ] Keep FTS index in sync with changes

## Dependencies
- Requires SQLite Storage Layer (FTS5 extension)
- Requires Schema & Migrations (FTS tables/triggers)
- Requires Model Types

## Rationale
Search is essential when the issue database grows large. Users need to find issues by remembered keywords, not just IDs. FTS5 provides stemming (finding "authenticate" when searching "authentication") and ranking.
